topic: SQLite database engine internals, API, performance tuning, and SQL dialect

expected_findings.description: Default page size is 4096 bytes since version 3.12.0
Database header string is 'SQLite format 3' with null terminator
Two types of B-trees: table B-trees (64-bit rowid key) and index B-trees
Page type flags: 2=interior index, 5=interior table, 10=leaf index, 13=leaf table
SQLite varint encoding uses between 1 and 9 bytes for 64-bit integers
Schema stored in sqlite_master table aliased as sqlite_schema since 3.33.0
WAL mode introduced in SQLite 3.7.0 allows concurrent readers and writer
Five lock states: UNLOCKED, SHARED, RESERVED, PENDING, EXCLUSIVE
WAL auto-checkpoint threshold default is 1000 pages
Rollback journal modes: DELETE, TRUNCATE, PERSIST, MEMORY, OFF
Five type affinities: TEXT, NUMERIC, INTEGER, REAL, BLOB
Type affinity determined by declared type containing INT, CHAR, BLOB, REAL, etc.
STRICT tables enforce type checking, allowed types: INT, INTEGER, REAL, TEXT, BLOB, ANY
WITHOUT ROWID tables stored as index B-trees, must have explicit PRIMARY KEY
RETURNING clause available since SQLite 3.35.0 for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
Maximum database size approximately 281 terabytes with default page size
UPSERT support via INSERT ... ON CONFLICT since version 3.24.0
Core workflow: sqlite3_open_v2, sqlite3_prepare_v2, sqlite3_step, sqlite3_finalize, sqlite3_close_v2
sqlite3_step returns SQLITE_ROW for each result row, SQLITE_DONE when finished
SQLITE_BUSY (code 5) returned when database is locked
Bind parameter index is 1-based; destructor can be SQLITE_STATIC or SQLITE_TRANSIENT
Default threading mode is serialized (SQLITE_THREADSAFE=1) with full mutex protection
Amalgamation is a single C file of ~155,000 lines compiling to ~750 KB
Memory-mapped I/O via PRAGMA mmap_size, disabled by default
Batch inserts in single transaction 50-100x faster than individual inserts
ANALYZE command gathers index statistics into sqlite_stat1 table for query planner
Covering indexes eliminate need to access table B-tree; shown as USING COVERING INDEX
Partial indexes only index rows matching a WHERE clause, since version 3.8.0
Multi-column index left-most prefix rule: index on (a,b,c) usable for (a), (a,b), (a,b,c)
Bloom filter optimization for joins introduced in SQLite 3.38.0
PRAGMA synchronous levels: OFF(0), NORMAL(1), FULL(2), EXTRA(3)
PRAGMA busy_timeout sets milliseconds to wait for lock; default returns SQLITE_BUSY immediately

expected_cross_references.description: Architecture describes WAL structure and journal modes; performance details WAL tuning and checkpointing
Architecture describes B-tree storage; SQL reference describes WITHOUT ROWID tables using index B-trees
C API defines threading modes and open flags; performance covers shared cache and busy handling

expected_gaps.description: No documentation on encryption or the SQLite Encryption Extension (SEE)
No documentation on FTS5 full-text search internals, tokenizers, or ranking
No documentation on the online backup API (sqlite3_backup_init, step, finish)
